CREATE TABLE registry (name VARCHAR(255) PRIMARY KEY, value VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO registry (name, value) VALUES ('test_key1', 'test_value1');
INSERT INTO registry (name, value) VALUES ('test_key2', 'test_value2');
UPDATE registry SET value = 'updated_value1' WHERE name = 'test_key1';
DELETE FROM registry WHERE name = 'test_key2';
SELECT * FROM registry WHERE name = 'test_key1';
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT value FROM registry WHERE name LIKE 'test_key%') AS temp;
CREATE TABLE protected_registry (name VARCHAR(255) PRIMARY KEY, value VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE);
INSERT INTO protected_registry (name, value) VALUES ('protected_key1', 'protected_value1');
SELECT * FROM registry WHERE name IN (SELECT name FROM protected_registry);
UPDATE protected_registry SET value = 'new_protected_value1' WHERE name = 'protected_key1';
WITH temp AS (SELECT * FROM registry WHERE value IS NOT NULL) SELECT COUNT(*) FROM temp;
SELECT value FROM registry WHERE CHAR_LENGTH(value) > 10;
INSERT INTO registry (name, value) SELECT CONCAT('key_', n), CONCAT('value_', n) FROM (SELECT ROW_NUMBER() OVER () AS n FROM dual) AS numbers WHERE n <= 100;
CREATE TABLE log (action VARCHAR(255), timestamp TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO log (action) VALUES ('Registry value set for test_key1');
SELECT name, value FROM registry WHERE value IS NOT NULL ORDER BY name DESC LIMIT 10;
INSERT INTO registry (name, value) VALUES (NULL, 'null_key_value');
INSERT INTO registry (name, value) VALUES ('key_with_special_chars@#$%', 'value_with_special_chars');
UPDATE registry SET value = 'updated_special_chars' WHERE name = 'key_with_special_chars@#$%';
